quick_skim.title: Distilling Safe LLM Systems via Soft Prompts for On Device Settings
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying safe large language models (LLMs) on resource-constrained edge devices presents a critical challenge: while dual-model systems combining LLMs with guard models provide effective safety guarantees, their substantial memory and computational demands make them prohibitively expensive for on-device deployment. This paper presents a comprehensive study of parameter-efficient safety alignment methods for resource-constrained settings. Through systematic evaluation across multiple LLM architectures, training objectives, and parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches, we identify that soft prompts combined with distillation-based training consistently outperform alternative methods. We introduce distillation frameworks based on total variation and KL divergence that effectively transfer safety behaviors from guard models into learned soft prompts. Our evaluations on various benchmarks demonstrate that this combination achieves superior safety-usefulness trade-offs compared to LoRA adapters, steering vectors, and direct optimization methods, while requiring minimal additional memory and compute at inference time. These findings establish soft prompt distillation as the preferred approach for safety alignment in on-device LLM deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: distillation-based safety alignment for on-device fine-tuning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于蒸馏的安全对齐方法用于设备端微调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于蒸馏的安全对齐方法用于设备端微调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于蒸馏的安全对齐方法用于设备端微调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes soft prompt distillation to achieve safe LLM deployment on edge devices via parameter-efficient fine-tuning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对资源受限设备上部署安全LLM的挑战，系统研究了参数高效安全对齐方法。通过多种LLM架构、训练目标和微调方式的比较，发现软提示结合蒸馏训练一致优于其他方案。提出了基于总变差和KL散度的蒸馏框架，在保持安全性的同时大幅降低内存和计算开销，为安全微调提供了一种轻量级解决方案。

quick_skim.title: GRASP: Geometry-aware Residual Alignment for Scalable Pretraining Data Attribution
quick_skim.abstract: Scalable data attribution methods typically assign isolated utility scores to individual training examples. This prevalent additive assumption fundamentally fails to capture critical subset dynamics, including data redundancy and complementary coverage. In this work, we reframe attribution as subset-level counterfactual utility prediction and introduce GRASP, an interaction-aware surrogate. Grounded in a theoretical smoothness lower bound, GRASP explicitly models subset interactions through a quadratic geometric penalty. To achieve pretraining-scale efficiency without relying on hidden oracle tuning, we couple low-dimensional feature sketches with a strictly finite lower-confidence bound selection protocol. Extensive subset-retraining evaluations demonstrate that GRASP decisively outperforms existing scalable baselines. It more than doubles the task-level rank correlation for counterfactual subset fidelity while reducing upfront artifact construction costs by nearly an order of magnitude. Downstream diagnostics further show that this scoring mechanism transfers to language model curation and cross-domain vision selection, establishing a robust foundation for optimizing massive pretraining corpora.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: subset-level data attribution method that could inform safety data selection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 子集级数据归因方法，可为安全数据选择提供指导
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 子集级数据归因方法，可为安全数据选择提供指导
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 子集级数据归因方法，可为安全数据选择提供指导
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GRASP reframes data attribution as subset-level counterfactual utility prediction, modeling interactions to inform data selection, with potential application to curating safety data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: GRASP提出将数据归因重新定义为子集级反事实效用预测，显式建模样本间交互作用，克服了加性假设的局限。该方法通过几何惩罚项捕捉子集动态，并采用低维特征草图实现预训练规模的高效计算。虽然面向通用数据归因，但其子集选择与交互建模能力可迁移至安全对齐数据的策划与混合场景，为安全数据选择提供理论指导。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找关于大语言模型安全微调中“安全对齐数据与普通微调数据混合”的相关论文。重点关注在微调过程中如何引入、选择、混合或调度安全对齐数据，以缓解微调导致的安全退化、有害微调攻击、越狱风险或安全对齐遗忘等问题。优先召回涉及 safety alignment data mixing、safety data selection、data curation、data scheduling、alignment preservation、harmful fine-tuning defense 等方向的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: mixing safety alignment data with fine-tuning data; selecting safety data for fine-tuning; curating safety data for alignment preservation; defending against harmful fine-tuning attacks; mixture of safety data and normal data in fine-tuning; mitigating safety degradation from fine-tuning; papers on mixing safety alignment data with standard fine tuning data to prevent alignment loss; research on data curation and scheduling for preserving model alignment during fine tuning; methods to defend against harmful fine tuning attacks by integrating safety data; how to select and incorporate safety data to mitigate jailbreak risks in LLM fine tuning.

quick_skim.title: DataEvolver: Automatic Data Preparation for Large Language Models through Multi-Level Self-Evolving
quick_skim.abstract: High-quality training data is essential to large language models (LLMs) and typically requires extensive and costly manual curation. Existing automatic data preparation methods rely on predefined pipelines or customized human instructions, which limits their adaptability to diverse data distributions and lacks principled guidance from high-quality examples. In this paper, we introduce DataEvolver, the first self-evolving data preparation system that automatically constructs pipelines to transform raw data into high-quality data. DataEvolver employs a multi-level mechanism to ensure both pipeline executability and effectiveness. At the operator level, it incrementally expands the operator set to construct a logical plan while resolving dependency conflicts. At the pipeline level, it instantiates logical plans into executable code and iteratively refines pipeline orchestration through a feedback loop that reduces the distribution gap between prepared data and high-quality examples. Experiments on seven benchmarks show that DataEvolver substantially improves data quality and achieves an average 10\% gain in downstream LLM performance compared with training on original data, highlighting new opportunities for the iterative co-evolution of LLMs and data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: automatic data preparation system that can be adapted for mixing safety data
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自动数据准备系统，可适用于混合安全数据
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自动数据准备系统，可适用于混合安全数据
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 自动数据准备系统，可适用于混合安全数据
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DataEvolver is a self-evolving system that automatically constructs pipelines to transform raw data into high-quality data, potentially applicable for curating and mixing safety alignment data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: DataEvolver提出第一个自进化数据准备系统，通过多级机制自动构建数据加工流水线，将原始数据转化为高质量数据。该系统可缓解手动策划的高成本问题，其灵活的数据变换能力可迁移至安全对齐数据与普通微调数据的混合与调度任务中。实验证明DataEvolver能有效提升数据质量，为安全数据策划提供了可扩展的自动化方案。

quick_skim.title: Securing Self-supervised Data Curation for Foundation Models Robustness
quick_skim.abstract: Self-supervised data curation provides a pathway to scaling and improving the generalization capabilities of machine learning models. By leveraging self-supervised learning (SSL) for data curation, the demand for massive training datasets required by foundation models can be effectively met. SSL greatly alleviates the costs associated with annotation and manual dataset curation while minimizing the need for human oversight. However, the integrity of SSL-curated datasets must be rigorously checked, as reliance on anonymous and unvetted external sources can substantially increase the risk of data poisoning. In this paper, we propose a Poisoned Data Detector (PDD), an active defense mechanism designed to ensure the integrity of SSL-curated datasets prior to foundation model training. PDDs are designed using a combination of the pretrained ImageBind model and traditional classifiers, including Random Forest (RF), k-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Naive Bayes (NB), and Support Vector Machines (SVM). We rigorously evaluated PDDs using 176,200 images from three diverse datasets and three different adversarial attacks encompassing both in-distribution and out-of-distribution scenarios. Notably, SVM-PDD achieves superior performance for both in-distribution (Set3-Set5) and out-of-distribution (TrueFace and 140K RealFace) datasets. Our design demonstrates strong scalability and enables the rapid integration of new adversarial attack detectors through an ensemble approach.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: secures data curation for foundation models against poisoning, relevant to safety data curation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 确保基础模型数据策展的完整性，防范投毒，与安全数据策展相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 确保基础模型数据策展的完整性，防范投毒，与安全数据策展相关

quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出毒化数据检测器（PDD），一种主动防御机制，用于确保自监督学习策展的数据集完整性。PDD在数据集用于训练基础模型前检测并清除潜在毒化样本，避免数据投毒攻击。该方法对安全对齐数据的策展具有启示意义，可用于保护微调数据免受恶意污染。

quick_skim.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
quick_skim.abstract: Supervised fine-tuning (SFT) typically maximizes the likelihood of every token in a demonstrated trajectory. However, an observed token can be non-unique, noisy, or misaligned with the model prior. Strictly fitting toward this one-hot target may be suboptimal, especially when the pretrained model encodes a rich knowledge prior. In this work, we reinterpret SFT as target distribution design: instead of studying only the loss objective, we analyze the token-level target that the loss drives the model to match. We introduce the Q-target framework, which decomposes SFT supervision into two explicit choices: (1) how strongly to rely on the observed token, and (2) how to allocate the remaining probability mass over alternatives. This perspective unifies many existing SFT variants as implicit choices of the target distribution Q. Building on this view, we propose Target-SFT which constructs the training objective directly from the desired target distribution. This method consistently outperforms across the ten reasoning dataset-model settings evaluated, showing the effectiveness of this target-based approach. Overall, our formulation reveals a more fundamental design principle for SFT training and opens a broader search space for SFT objectives.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: framework for target distribution design in SFT, applicable to safety data mixing
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: SFT目标分布设计框架，可应用于安全数据混合
quick_skim.llm_evidence: SFT目标分布设计框架，可应用于安全数据混合
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: SFT目标分布设计框架，可应用于安全数据混合
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Q-target framework for designing token-level targets in SFT, enabling principled allocation of probability mass to safe alternatives.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文重新解释监督微调为目标分布设计，提出Q-target框架，将微调监督分解为对观察token的依赖程度和剩余概率分配。该框架统一了多种现有方法，并可用于设计安全敏感的训练目标，例如在安全数据混合时控制对安全token的信任度。
